metadata.purpose: System-wide conceptual questions requiring synthesis across multiple modules

questions.question: What is the core purpose of plc-gbt and how does it provide a novel solution to process control and automation version control?
questions.answer: plc-gbt enables GitHub-based version control for PLC programs by using a custom .ACD to .L5X conversion tool (acd-l5x-tool-lib) that converts proprietary and encrypted Rockwell .ACD files into XML-based .L5X format, making them diff-friendly and trackable in git repositories.
source_hints: repository topics: plc, l5x, acd, rockwell

questions.question: Why is .ACD to .L5X conversion essential for modern DevOps practices in industrial automation?
questions.answer: .ACD files are proprietary binary archives that cannot be diffed or merged. .L5X is XML-based, enabling meaningful diffs, pull request reviews, merge conflict resolution, and CI/CD pipeline integration - bridging industrial automation with modern software development practices.

questions.question: How does plc-gbt integrate AI assistance with traditional PLC programming workflows?
questions.answer: plc-gbt uses a fine-tuned OpenAI model (gpt-4o-mini) with domain-specific system prompts emphasizing TRUTH-SEEKING, CONCISE, and HONEST responses. It ingests PLC program context into a Neo4j knowledge graph for RAG-based retrieval, enabling AI to understand control loop structures, tuning parameters, and provide contextual assistance.

questions.question: What is the control loop classification system in plc-gbt and why does it distinguish between ladder logic and function block implementations?
questions.answer: plc-gbt classifies PID controllers into four types: ladder_logic_standard_pid, ladder_logic_advanced_pid, function_block_standard_pide, function_block_advanced_pide. This distinction is necessary because ladder logic (older, rung-based) and function block (modern, object-oriented PIDE instruction) implementations have different tuning parameters, configuration options, and behaviors.

questions.question: How does the cascade control configuration in plc-gbt enable complex multi-loop control strategies?
questions.answer: Cascade control connects master and slave loops where the master's output becomes the slave's setpoint. plc-gbt supports four communication methods (direct_write, message_instruction, produced_tag, ethernet_ip), configurable mode coordination (cascade_fault_action), and separate tuning parameters for master (overshoot_limit, response_time_target) and slave (response_ratio, stability_margin) loops.

questions.question: What safety measures does plc-gbt implement to prevent accidental PLC modifications?
questions.answer: plc-gbt enforces READ_ONLY connection mode by default for safety. Write operations require explicit ADMIN permission level, validated through the @requires_permission decorator. The CLI uses a session-based configuration with timeout (3600s default) and supports multiple validation levels (MINIMAL, BASIC, STANDARD, ADVANCED) including simulation testing before deployment.

questions.question: How does plc-gbt's n8n integration enable workflow automation for industrial processes?
questions.answer: plc-gbt integrates with n8n workflow automation to create event-driven workflows for PLC monitoring, alerting, and automated responses. The n8n-mcp module provides Model Context Protocol integration, while the n8n-framework enables visual workflow design connecting PLC events to notifications, data logging, and external system triggers.

questions.question: How does plc-gbt structure its JSON Schema definitions to support multiple PID controller configurations?
questions.answer: plc-gbt organizes schemas in /schemas/control-loops/subtypes/ with four subdirectories: cascade, feedforward, combined_ff_cascade, and multi_formula_weighted_ff. Each subtype has variants for all four controller types (ladder standard/advanced, function block standard/advanced). Schemas use JSON Schema 2020-12 draft with $ref for composition and reuse.

questions.question: What is the instance lifecycle in plc-gbt and how does it track PLC program states?
questions.answer: Instances progress through states: CONFIGURED (initial) → RUNNING → PAUSED/STOPPED/ERROR. The InstanceStatus enum defines seven states, and transitions are controlled by the CLI with permission checks. Each instance has a unique ID, associated control loop type, validation level, and maintains a history of state changes for audit purposes.

questions.question: How does the plc-gbt UI implement a flexible, user-customizable layout system?
questions.answer: The UI uses Zustand for state management with localStorage persistence (key: plc-gbt-layout-storage). The useLayoutStore supports seven tool types, three layout presets (minimal, development, debugging), and drag-and-drop reordering via @dnd-kit. The ControlLoopGrid uses responsive Tailwind CSS grid classes for adaptive layouts.

questions.question: How does plc-gbt ensure data integrity for file storage operations?
questions.answer: FileStorageService uses SHA-256 checksums (calculate_checksum method) to verify file integrity. It implements soft deletes via deleted_at column, chunked processing (8192 byte default chunk_size), and atomic operations. The API maintains command history (max 1000 results) for audit trails.

questions.question: What PLC controller families does plc-gbt support and what are their key capability differences?
questions.answer: plc-gbt supports three Rockwell/Allen-Bradley controller families: ControlLogix (1000 programs, 250K tags, motion support, 128 I/O modules), CompactLogix (100 programs, 50K tags, limited motion), and Micro800 (10 programs, 1000 tags, no motion/safety). Each has different capacity limits affecting program structure and validation.

questions.question: How does plc-gbt bridge the gap between process control engineers and software developers?
questions.answer: plc-gbt translates PLC concepts into software development paradigms: .ACD→.L5X conversion enables git workflows, JSON Schemas standardize control loop definitions, the CLI provides familiar command-line interfaces, React/Next.js UI offers modern dashboards, and AI assistance explains PLC-specific concepts. This enables collaboration using shared tooling.
source_hints: Multiple: CLI, UI, schemas, AI integration

questions.question: What validation levels does plc-gbt provide and when would you use each?
questions.answer: Four ValidationLevels: MINIMAL (syntax only, for quick checks), BASIC (syntax + structure, for development), STANDARD (includes controller capability validation, for staging), ADVANCED (adds simulation testing, for production deployment). Higher levels catch more issues but take longer to execute.

questions.question: How does the plc-gbt analytics dashboard support real-time monitoring of control loops?
questions.answer: The analytics-store supports four widget types (chart, metric, table, gauge) for visualizing control loop performance. ControlLoopStats displays health indicators with color-coded thresholds (green/yellow/red via Tailwind classes). React Query provides data fetching with configurable staleTime (5min) and cacheTime (10min) for efficient updates.
